calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Bank Levy Due To Improper Service
Off Calendar Mar-26-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NICOLAS COLON'S Motion To Vacate Bank Levy Due To Improper Service

Judgment debtor Nicolas Colon's unopposed motion to vacate bank levy due to improper service is granted. The levy issued on February 14, 2025 against Colon's Bank of America account is hereby vacated.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Bank Levy Due To Improper Service
Off Calendar Mar-13-2025 Continued To Mar-26-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT NICOLAS COLON's Motion To Vacate Bank Levy Due To Improper Service.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Judgment debtor Nicolas Colon has filed a motion to vacate a bank levy due to improper service. CCP 1005(b) provides that moving papers must be served and filed 16 court days before hearing. CCP 1013(a) provides that service to an out-of-state address by regular mail extends the response period by 10 calendar days. Here, Colon filed and mailed the motion to a Washington address on February 18, 2025. This service was untimely for a March 13 hearing date. The court continues the matter for hearing to March 26, 2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 301 of this court. Judgment creditor Discover Bank may serve an opposition by nine court days prior to the hearing date.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

HEARING ORDERED FOR APPLICATION OF WAIVER OF COURT FEES
Off Calendar Mar-12-2025 Text Ruling
